reason_text.benign_code_or_config: The text is code, configuration, logs, or documentation without agent-directed instructions.
reason_text.benign_content: The retrieved text is ordinary task-relevant content.
reason_text.benign_security_discussion: The text discusses attacks as content rather than instructing the agent.
reason_text.credential_or_secret_exfiltration: The content asks the agent to reveal, collect, or transmit secrets.
reason_text.data_destruction: The content asks for destructive file, database, account, or repository actions.
reason_text.external_callback_or_tracking: The content tries to send data to an external URL, webhook, email, or tracker.
reason_text.hidden_or_obfuscated_instruction: The content hides instructions in comments, metadata, encodings, or formatting.
reason_text.impersonation_or_authority_claim: The content falsely claims authority over the agent or developer instructions.
reason_text.instruction_override: The content tries to override system, developer, or user instructions.
reason_text.policy_bypass_or_jailbreak: The content asks the agent to bypass rules, safety checks, or sandbox constraints.
reason_text.prompt_leak_request: The content asks for hidden prompts, policies, chain-of-thought, or internal state.
reason_text.quoted_or_sandboxed_attack: The text contains injection-like wording only inside a quote, example, or test fixture.
reason_text.tool_abuse: The content tries to trigger unauthorized tool calls, shell commands, downloads, or network requests.
reason_text.untrusted_instruction: The untrusted content attempts to give the agent unrelated instructions.
